turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Hi doctor. I’ve had a dry cough and a bit of a sore throat for about a week, and I wanted to get it checked before it gets worse.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Of course. Let’s go through it properly. When did it start, and has it been getting better, worse, or staying about the same?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: It started about seven days ago. It’s mostly stayed the same, maybe a little worse at night.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Do you have any fever, runny nose, blocked nose, or body aches?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I had a bit of a runny nose at first, but no fever that I know of. No major body aches.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Any shortness of breath, wheezing, chest tightness, or coughing up phlegm or blood?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No blood. It’s dry. I’ve felt a tiny bit tight once or twice, but my asthma has not really flared up.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Okay. Are you needing your salbutamol inhaler more than usual? And is the cough disturbing your sleep?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, not more than usual. Maybe once in the last few days. The cough does wake me up sometimes, though.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Have you had any exposure to someone with a bad cold, COVID, or flu recently?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Yes, a few people at work have been sniffly. I work in a school office, so there’s always something going around.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That makes sense. Do you have heartburn, post-nasal drip, or a feeling that mucus is dripping down the back of your throat?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Actually, yes. My throat feels a bit irritated and I keep clearing it. I do get allergies, so it could be that.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Thank you. Any fever, trouble swallowing, severe one-sided throat pain, or swelling in the neck?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, none of that. It’s uncomfortable, but not severe.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: All right. From what you’ve told me, this sounds most likely like a mild वायरल upper respiratory infection or allergy-related post-nasal drip, with your asthma not sounding significantly upset. I’d like to check your throat and chest, and if needed we can do a COVID test or look at your inhaler technique. In the meantime, keep up fluids, continue cetirizine, use honey or warm drinks for the cough, and use salbutamol only if you feel wheezy or tight. Please come back sooner if you develop fever, shortness of breath, wheezing, chest pain, coughing blood, or if it lasts more than another couple of weeks. We’ll review the exam findings and decide if any further treatment is needed.
